ads.title: Pinball machine Hokus Pokus 1975 owned since 1979
ads.description: Edit March 20 We moved into new place and something happened along the way where it is no longer resetting. When you hit the reset button it makes a noise like it's going from one to two player but nothing resets. I know it's something minor. I have lowered the price considerably. Had every switch , blade and contact filed and cleaned and machine shopped out in 2021 by Terri Irwin , a long time respected pinball mechanic in Ocala. Has a few lights out. Only issue is sometimes the score reel does not completely set over and you have to hit the button again to restart it. One of those Ghost in the machine kind of things that she just couldn't figure out . I have owned this game since 1979 when I received it as a kid. You might have seen this up for sale in the past and then the listing disappeared. This was a period of time when I needed money for something so I put it up for sale but then something else came through and I took it down because I didn't really want to sell it. At this point in life it is mostly just nostalgia. I just don't play it anymore. It is very fun game I am just bored with it after 45 years and want something different. Very open to trades for a newer pinball machine plus cash on my end. Thanks for looking! Nine zero four four four four one four three nine.. Dave

ads.author: Kurt Stricker
ads.title: Vintage Pub Time Darts Electronic Dartboard Arcade Machine by Merit Industries - Beauty!
ads.description: Step back into the golden age of arcade fun with this authentic Pub Time Darts machine! This is the real deal – a classic 1980s Merit Industries Pub Time Darts electronic dartboard that’s been the life of the party in countless bars, game rooms, and man caves. The full-size cabinet features that iconic colorful graphics, the massive regulation-style dartboard display with all the vibrant red, blue, and gold detailing, player stations for up to 4 players (or 8 with competition mode), and the classic “throw darts” vibe complete with beer-mug branding. Condition highlights (being 100% transparent): Everything lights up beautifully – marquee, dartboard segments, buttons, score displays, and overhead lighting all glow bright and vibrant with that perfect retro arcade feel. Looks fantastic on the porch, in a game room, or tucked into a home bar setup. Game selection currently does not work (you can’t pick a game right now). This is a super common issue on these vintage Merit boards and usually comes down to a simple board-level repair, loose connection, or matrix issue that electronics hobbyists fix all the time. Parts are readily available online if you want to bring it back to full glory. Whether you’re a collector, a tinkerer who loves restoring arcade classics, or just want an awesome conversation piece that screams “Florida backyard bar,” this machine has serious character. Imagine firing up some tunes, grabbing a cold one, and turning your space into a mini pub night after night. Specs & Details: Full upright cabinet (approx. 7–8 ft tall when assembled) Coin-op ready (can be set to free play) Soft-tip darts style (safe & fun) Multiple game modes when working (301, Cricket, etc.) Heavy-duty commercial build – these things last forever

ads.author: Tim Timmins
ads.title: Galaga Video Arcade Game Machine ORIGINAL *SPARE WORKING BOARDSET INCLUDED* White "Bosconian"
ads.description: Here's an original Galaga arcade game machine. This is the real deal from back in the day, not some toy you buy at Walmart. ***This game also comes with a complete spare, working Galaga boardset - cage and all.*** This is the "white" version of Galaga - Midway was making a game called Bosconian at the time Galaga was released and Galaga became a runaway hit. So, Midway took many of the Bosconian cabinets (which had white sides) they had at the factory and converted them to Galaga to meet demand. If you look closely, you can even see the Bosconian sideart underneath the Galaga artwork. The machine is running all original hardware and passes the boot-up self-test just fine. All switches, buttons, lights work as they should - yes, even the coin door lights! The cabinet is in overall good shape with expected wear. The sideart is original and has some dings, etc. The kickplate art is better than most. The bezel is in very good shape, with virtually no visible flaking and the marquee is also very strong. The screen is original and has some standard Galaga burn but the smoked plexi does its job well so you can't see it during gameplay. The original control panel overlay is present and has some typical cracking/wear along the front edge. The panel was stripped of its parts, which were disassembled and washed. The joystick shaft was also buffed so it's silverish again as it should be. The overlay got the Awesome! and Magic Eraser treatment so the colors look good and sharp, as do the whites. The coin door is in fine cosmetic shape but the lock is for looks only; it doesn't have a key. The coin door's internal self-test and operator's credit button are not functional as they don't appear to have wires for them which is odd. The game can still be coined up by flicking the coin mech switch inside the coin door and it's just as easy. The game can also take quarters as the coin mechs are present as is the original coin box. Lastly, the original back door as well as the original manual and schematics are included. Cash and carry in Plano, TX. Play before you pay. First floor, no stairs and I can help you load! :D
